name: Archangels (Michael, Gabriel, Raphael, Uriel)
shortDescription: Michael, Gabriel, Raphael, and Uriel are considered the four primary archangels in various Christian traditions, each associated with distinct roles and responsibilities as messengers and warriors of God.
description: Michael, Gabriel, Raphael, and Uriel hold significant positions within Christian angelology, though their prominence and acceptance vary across different denominations. Michael, whose name means "Who is like God?", is often depicted as the leader of the heavenly hosts and a warrior against evil, notably in the Book of Revelation. He is seen as a protector of the Church and a champion of God's people. Gabriel, meaning "God is my strength", is primarily known as the messenger of God, most famously delivering the news of Jesus's impending birth to Mary in the Gospel of Luke. He is often associated with communication, revelation, and guidance. Raphael, meaning "God heals", is the archangel of healing and is featured in the Book of Tobit, where he guides and protects Tobit's son, Tobias, and heals Tobit's blindness. He is invoked for healing and protection during travels. Uriel, meaning "God is my light", is considered one of the seven archangels in some traditions, but his canonicity is debated. He is often associated with wisdom, knowledge, and prophecy, and is said to have warned Noah of the impending flood in some apocryphal texts. While the Bible explicitly names only Michael and Gabriel, traditions and interpretations developed over centuries have established the significance of these four archangels in Christian belief and practice. The reverence and acceptance of these archangels vary among Christian denominations, with some emphasizing their roles more than others. Their stories and attributes have been elaborated upon in both canonical and apocryphal texts, contributing to a rich tapestry of angelic lore within Christianity.
